title: Software
text: I develop research software for scalable Bayesian inference and statistical machine learning. My packages are built to turn methodological research into reusable tools, with performance-critical components implemented in Rcpp.

title: Software
text:
Packages

Geometric Gaussian Processes on Manifolds
FLGP is an R package for scalable Bayesian inference of heat kernel Gaussian processes on manifolds.

Implements reduced-rank approximations for scalable Gaussian process inference.
Targets large, high-dimensional scientific data while preserving intrinsic geometry.
Written with Rcpp for performance-sensitive computation.



Bayesian Model Selection
ABMRS is an R package for adaptive Bayesian multivariate spline knot inference.

Supports Bayesian model selection with priors on model complexity.
Designed for flexible multivariate spline modeling.
Written with Rcpp for efficient computation.

title: Junhui He
text:
I am a PhD student in Statistics at Tsinghua University and a visiting PhD student in Biostatistics at the University of Michigan. I build statistical machine learning methods for high-dimensional scientific data, with a focus on Bayesian inference, Gaussian processes, manifold learning, and scalable algorithms.
I am especially interested in industry roles where scalable computation, reliable software, and modern machine learning meet: large language models, data science, applied scientist, machine learning engineer, quantitative research, and AI for science. My recent work includes scalable Bayesian inference on manifolds, spatiotemporal modeling for transcriptomics, and statistical methods for imaging and biomedical data.
hejh22@mails.tsinghua.edu.cn

title: Junhui He
text:
Selected Highlights

Published scalable Bayesian inference research in JRSSB, reducing heat-kernel Gaussian process computation from cubic to linear complexity for large-scale data on Riemannian manifolds.
Developed research software packages in R/Rcpp for Gaussian process modeling and Bayesian model selection.
Applied statistical modeling to neuroimaging and transcriptomics.
Trained in mathematics and statistics at Tsinghua University, with ongoing biostatistics research experience at the University of Michigan.

title: Junhui He
text:
Technical Skills

Programming languages: R, Python, C++
Statistical modeling: Bayesian inference, Gaussian processes, Markov chain Monte Carlo, variational inference, spline models
Machine learning: PyTorch, scikit-learn, manifold learning, dimensionality reduction
Scientific computing: Rcpp, graph Laplacians, reduced-rank approximation, high-dimensional data analysis
Application areas: neuroimaging, transcriptomics, biomedical imaging, quantitative finance

title: Junhui He
text:
Research & Software Experience
Scalable Bayesian inference for heat kernel Gaussian processes on manifolds

Developed scalable Bayesian methodology for manifold-valued data using graph Laplacian approximations and reduced-rank computation.
Reduced computational complexity from \(O(n^3)\) to \(O(n)\) for large-scale inference while preserving intrinsic data geometry.
Built the accompanying FLGP R package with Rcpp implementation.

Adaptive Bayesian multivariate spline model selection

Developed Bayesian model selection methods for multivariate spline knot inference with priors on model complexity.
Built the accompanying ABMRS R package with Rcpp implementation.

Applied biomedical data modeling

Worked on statistical methods for spatiotemporal transcriptomics and imaging data.
Contributed to optical and statistical analysis of skin reflectance measurement using large-scale spectra data.

Quantitative research internship, XY Investments, Shanghai | 2021

Applied mathematical and statistical modeling in a quantitative research setting.

title: Junhui He
text:
Publications


He, Junhui, Guoxuan Ma, Jian Kang, and Ying Yang. 2026. “Scalable Bayesian Inference for Heat Kernel Gaussian Processes on Manifolds.” Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B: Statistical Methodology 88 (2): 516–39. https://doi.org/10.1093/jrsssb/qkaf057.


He, Junhui, Ying Yang, and Jian Kang. 2024. “Adaptive Bayesian Multivariate Spline Knot Inference with Prior Specifications on Model Complexity.” https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13353.


Pryor, Yemko, Junhui He, Jian Kang, Blair Jenkins, and Tina Lasisi. 2026. “Optical Limits in Skin Reflectance Measurement: Quantifying Melanin-Dependent Constraints on Erythema Detection.” Journal of Investigative Dermatology. https://doi.org/https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jid.2026.03.025.
